example.reasoning: The Bhattacharyya coefficient is calculated as the sum of element-wise square roots of the product of p and q, giving BC = 0.8898. The distance is then -log(0.8898) = 0.1166.

title: Bhattacharyya Distance Between Two Distributions

pytorch_test_cases.test:
import torch
p = torch.tensor([0.1, 0.2, 0.3, 0.4])
q = torch.tensor([0.4, 0.3, 0.2, 0.1])
result = bhattacharyya_distance(p, q)
print(result)
